name: 16-chunk-boundary-split
description: Canonical scenario #16: SSE chunk boundary splits a JSON field. The emulator emits one delta-bearing event whose serialized bytes are split across two TCP writes at an unfortunate boundary (mid-string), separated by a setImmediate flush so the kernel send buffer actually fragments. After both writes arrive the event is well-formed; the SDK's incremental SSE/JSON parser MUST reassemble it and produce the same canonical text output a non-split stream would. This is the SDK-incremental-parser parity test: it is expected to PASS with the reassembled event, and the comparator uses exact-mode positional equality on the canonical event stream. Per issue body ambiguity call #3 — if either SDK fails this scenario, that's evidence of an incremental-parser bug worth filing upstream. The failure injection is unusual in that it goes through the failure code path (entry.kind=failure, split_json_field) but the resulting stream is well-formed end-to-end including the [DONE] terminator; that asymmetry is deliberate so a single emulator code path covers both 'parser must reassemble' tests AND 'parser must surface an error' tests.
systemPrompt: You are a helpful assistant. Answer concisely.
